---
title: "Bias-corrected meta-analysis model"
vignette: >
  %\VignetteIndexEntry{bias-corrected-jung-verde}
  %\VignetteEngine{quarto::html}
  %\VignetteEncoding{UTF-8}
---

## Introduction

The bias-corrected model accounts for systematic bias in some studies.

This approach extends the model proposed by Verde and later developed by Jung.

## Model

The observed effect is

\[
y_i \sim \mathcal{N}(\theta_i^B, s_i^2)
\]

where

\[
\theta_i^B =
(1 - I_i)\theta_i + I_i(\theta_i + \beta_i)
\]

- \(I_i\) indicates whether study \(i\) is biased
- \(\beta_i\) represents the bias magnitude

## Hierarchical structure

\[
\theta_i \sim \mathcal{N}(\mu, \tau^2)
\]

\[
\beta_i \sim \mathcal{N}(\mu_\beta, \tau_\beta^2)
\]

### Interpretation

The model separates **true effects** from **bias effects**.
